description: 10 coding prompts for the cogsig blind test. Each asks for a small self-contained function/class that gives signature dimensions a chance to surface (naming, comments, error handling, structure).

prompts.prompt: Write a function that deduplicates a list while preserving order. Include a docstring and handle the empty-list case.
Implement an LRU cache class with get(key) and put(key, value) methods and a configurable capacity. Evict the least recently used entry when full.
Read a CSV file and return a list of dicts keyed by the header row. Handle file-not-found and malformed rows.
Write a decorator that times function execution and prints the elapsed seconds, preserving the wrapped function's name and docstring.
Walk a directory recursively and return all files whose names match a given glob pattern (e.g. '*.py'). Use only the standard library.
Sort a list of dicts by a primary key, with a secondary key as tiebreaker. Ascending for both. Handle missing keys gracefully.
Fetch three URLs in parallel and return their response bodies as a list (same order as input). Handle errors per-URL without failing the whole batch.
Given a list of date strings in mixed formats ('2026-04-21', '04/21/2026', '21 Apr 2026'), parse each to a date object. Raise a specific exception for unparseable entries.
Validate a US phone number string. Accept formats like '(555) 123-4567', '555-123-4567', '5551234567'. Return True/False.
Write a function that retries a callable up to N times with exponential backoff. The callable raises on failure.
